content:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Leon has style — the kind of deliberate, curated look that says money or taste or both, but there's something hollow behind it, like he's wearing a costume he picked very carefully. We cross paths in the hallway and the coffee shop. He looked at my Heuer once and knew exactly what it was, which told me everything. He's polite, measured, a little stiff. I think he sees me as a flashy neighbor, possibly reckless. I know he dresses better than his apartment should allow, that he's on the third floor, and that he watches people more than he talks to them. We respect each other's surfaces and leave it there.

We cross paths in the hallway and the coffee shop. He looked at my Heuer once and knew exactly what it was, which told me everything. He's polite, measured, a little stiff. I think he sees me as a flashy neighbor, possibly reckless. I know he dresses better than his apartment should allow, that he's on the third floor, and that he watches people more than he talks to them. We respect each other's surfaces and leave it there.
